disclaimer: Governance working analysis; legal counsel should confirm final wording.

items.title: Modernize governing-authority and precedence clause
items.current: The bylaws state only that they must conform to the 1974 Charter.
items.needsWork: Add ONCA and its regulations to the authority hierarchy and state what prevails if documents conflict.
evidenceRequired: Certified copy of current articles/1974 Charter
Board and member resolution adopting amended bylaw
Final signed bylaw and Ontario filing confirmation if articles are changed
items.suggestedWording: These By-laws are subject to the Not-for-Profit Corporations Act, 2010 (Ontario), the regulations made under it, and the articles of the Corporation, including the Charter dated December 20, 1974, as amended. If there is any inconsistency, the Act and regulations prevail, followed by the articles, these By-laws, and then approved policies and resolutions.
items.task: Draft and present an ONCA authority and precedence amendment.

items.title: Create definitions, interpretation and severability article
items.current: No general definitions or interpretation article exists.
items.needsWork: Define Act, Board, Corporation, Director, Member, Officer, writing and electronic means; add severability and gender-neutral interpretation.
evidenceRequired: Committee comparison against Ontario standard organizational bylaw
items.suggestedWording: In these By-laws, “Act” means the Not-for-Profit Corporations Act, 2010 (Ontario) and its regulations, as amended. Defined terms have the meanings assigned in the Act. Words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa. The invalidity of one provision does not affect the remaining provisions.
items.task: Create a new Definitions, Interpretation and Severability article.

items.title: Add a statutory annual meeting framework
items.current: Monthly meetings are required, but no meeting is expressly designated as the annual meeting of members with mandatory annual business.
items.needsWork: Designate an annual meeting and list required business, notice, financial statements, elections and public accountant decisions.
compliesWith: ONCA s. 84 — financial statements to members
items.suggestedWording: The Corporation shall hold an annual meeting of members within the period required by the Act. At the annual meeting, members shall receive the financial statements and any auditor’s or review engagement report, elect directors, appoint a public accountant or approve any lawful exemption, and transact other business properly brought before the meeting.
items.task: Draft an annual meeting amendment and annual-meeting checklist.

items.title: Complete electronic meeting, voting and notice rules
items.current: Virtual attendance and email notice are mentioned, but identity, participation, voting, technical interruption and notice instructions are not addressed.
items.needsWork: Create complete electronic meeting rules and a supporting policy.
compliesWith: O. Reg. 395/21 s. 10 — notice by electronic means
items.suggestedWording: A meeting may be held entirely or partly by telephonic or electronic means that permit all participants to communicate adequately. The notice shall include access and voting instructions. The Chair shall establish reasonable procedures for identity verification, voting, technical interruptions and recording attendance.
items.task: Create electronic meetings and electronic voting amendment and policy.

items.article: Article 7 — Elected Officers / Board of Directors
items.title: Add director qualifications, consent, removal and resignation rules
items.current: The bylaws cover election, term and vacancies but not the full statutory director lifecycle.
items.needsWork: State qualifications, written consent, resignation, removal by members, loss of qualification and vacancy rules.
items.suggestedWording: Each Director shall satisfy the qualifications in the Act and provide written consent to act. A Director may resign in writing. Members may remove a Director in accordance with the Act. A vacancy shall be filled only in a manner permitted by the Act and the Corporation shall maintain an accurate directors’ register.
items.task: Draft a complete director lifecycle amendment and onboarding form.

items.title: Add statutory duties and standard of care
items.current: Officer job descriptions exist, but statutory fiduciary duties and the standard of care are not stated.
items.needsWork: Add duties to act honestly, in good faith and in the Corporation’s best interests, and the prudent-person standard.
compliesWith: ONCA s. 43 — duties of directors and officers
items.suggestedWording: Every Director and Officer shall act honestly and in good faith with a view to the best interests of the Corporation and shall exercise the care, diligence and skill that a reasonably prudent person would exercise in comparable circumstances, in accordance with the Act.
items.task: Draft statutory duties amendment and annual acknowledgement.

items.title: Create conflict-of-interest article and disclosure workflow
items.current: No conflict-of-interest article or meeting procedure exists despite honorariums, contracts and investment decisions.
items.needsWork: Require written disclosure, abstention, non-participation where applicable and minute documentation.
compliesWith: ONCA s. 41 — disclosure of conflict of interest
items.suggestedWording: A Director or Officer who is a party to, has a material interest in, or is connected to a material contract or transaction shall disclose the nature and extent of the interest in writing as required by the Act, shall not vote on the matter, and shall comply with all participation restrictions under the Act. The disclosure and disposition shall be recorded in the minutes.
items.task: Create a Conflict of Interest article, declaration form and meeting prompt.

items.article: New Article — Corporate Records and Member Access
items.title: Create statutory corporate-records article and retention policy
items.current: Secretary and Treasurer duties mention minutes and financial records but do not create a complete statutory records system.
items.needsWork: List required records, custodians, access rights, retention, privacy and secure disposal.
compliesWith: ONCA Part IX, ss. 92–98 — records and access
ONCA s. 84 — member financial statement access
items.suggestedWording: The Corporation shall prepare and maintain all records required by the Act, including its articles and By-laws, minutes and resolutions of members, directors and committees, registers of directors, officers and members, and adequate accounting records. Access shall be provided in accordance with the Act and an approved records-retention and privacy policy.
items.task: Create Corporate Records and Member Access article plus retention schedule.

items.article: Article 10–11 and Appendix — Budget, Financial and Auditors
items.title: Replace internal “Auditors” language with ONCA financial-review framework
items.current: Two members act as internal Auditors. This may be a valuable internal control but is not automatically an ONCA audit or review engagement.
items.needsWork: Determine public-benefit status and annual revenue thresholds each year; distinguish internal review from statutory audit/review engagement; document lawful waiver where available.
compliesWith: ONCA Part VII, ss. 68–84 — public accountant and financial statements
evidenceRequired: Member resolution appointing public accountant or lawful waiver
items.suggestedWording: The Corporation shall prepare annual financial statements and shall appoint an auditor or person to conduct a review engagement, or approve an exemption, only as permitted by the Act. Any internal financial review committee is an additional control and shall not be represented as satisfying a statutory audit or review engagement unless legally qualified and appointed.
items.task: Draft financial statements and public accountant amendment; rename internal Auditors committee.

items.article: Article 3 and Article 5 — Voting and Quorum
items.title: Clarify chair voting and strengthen quorum
items.current: The presiding officer casts a deciding vote in a tie, and member quorum is only three.
items.needsWork: Clarify whether the chair has an ordinary vote, casting vote or both; consider a higher or percentage-based member quorum; verify board quorum against board size.
compliesWith: ONCA member meeting and director meeting rules
items.suggestedWording: The Chair may vote as a member if otherwise entitled but shall not have an additional casting vote unless expressly permitted by the Act and these By-laws. Quorum for a meeting of members shall be [number or percentage] of voting members, subject to the Act.
items.task: Review and amend chair voting and quorum provisions.

items.article: Article 4 and Article 6 — Membership and Dues
items.title: Add member discipline, termination and procedural fairness
items.current: Membership eligibility and dues arrears are addressed, but no general discipline, suspension or termination procedure exists.
items.needsWork: Add grounds, notice, opportunity to respond, impartial decision and written outcome; reconcile automatic resignation language with ONCA and the Charter.
compliesWith: ONCA s. 51 — discipline and termination procedures
items.suggestedWording: A member may be disciplined, suspended or terminated only in accordance with a fair and reasonable procedure approved by the Board and consistent with the Act. The member shall receive written notice of the proposed action and reasons, a reasonable opportunity to respond, and written notice of the decision.
items.task: Create member discipline and termination article and procedure.

items.title: Create indemnification and directors’ insurance article
items.current: Insurance committee duties exist, but there is no director/officer indemnification clause.
items.needsWork: Authorize indemnification and insurance only within ONCA limits and connect this to annual insurance review.
items.suggestedWording: Subject to the Act, the Corporation may indemnify a Director, Officer or other eligible person and may purchase and maintain insurance for their benefit. No indemnity shall be provided where the statutory conditions are not met.

items.title: Reconcile amendment procedure with ONCA
items.current: The current process requires notice at one meeting and a two-thirds vote at the next, but does not describe the directors’ authority and member confirmation process under ONCA.
items.needsWork: State the lawful process for board-made bylaws, member confirmation, rejection and effective dates; preserve any desired enhanced notice threshold only where lawful.
compliesWith: ONCA s. 17 — bylaws and member confirmation
items.suggestedWording: The Board may make, amend or repeal By-laws in accordance with the Act. Any such By-law, amendment or repeal shall be submitted to the members for confirmation, rejection or amendment within the time required by the Act. Enhanced notice or voting requirements in these By-laws apply only to the extent consistent with the Act.
items.task: Draft an ONCA-compliant bylaw amendment procedure.

items.article: New Article — Member Rights and Proposals
items.title: Create member rights, access and proposal article
items.current: The bylaws describe voting eligibility but do not summarize statutory inspection, financial statement, proposal and meeting rights.
items.needsWork: Add a clear rights article without attempting to restrict mandatory ONCA rights.
compliesWith: ONCA ss. 84, 95–98 — financial statements and records access
items.suggestedWording: Members have the rights provided by the Act, including rights relating to meetings, voting, proposals, financial statements and access to corporate records. Nothing in these By-laws shall restrict a right that the Act makes mandatory. Requests shall be processed and documented in accordance with an approved member-rights procedure.
items.task: Create Member Rights and Proposals article and request workflow.

items.title: Remove or revalidate outdated tax and filing guidance
items.current: The appendix relies on a 2021 phone conversation and historical Schedule 546 forms.
items.needsWork: Confirm current CRA and Ontario Business Registry filing requirements; move tax guidance out of bylaws into a controlled compliance procedure with review dates.
compliesWith: Ontario Business Registry annual return requirements
evidenceRequired: Controlled procedure approval and next review date
items.suggestedWording: Tax and corporate filing procedures shall be maintained as a separate controlled compliance policy and reviewed annually. Historical forms and informal advice shall not be treated as current legal requirements.
items.task: Verify current tax and corporate filing obligations and remove outdated appendix guidance.
